evals.name: deposit-starter-songbook-fujara
evals.prompt: I just finished building a fujara from the build packet at https://github.com/tonykoop/fujara. Now I want music to play on it. Drop a learn-to-play/ folder into that repo with three beginner public-domain tunes that fit the fujara's overtone-series character, two intermediate tunes, one Heifer Zephyr original written for the fujara, and a printable songsheet PDF. Use the sheet-music skill at C:\Users\Tony\Documents\GitHub\sheet-music.
evals.expected_output: A learn-to-play/ folder inside the fujara repo containing 00-warmup-scales/, 01-easy/ (3 tunes), 02-intermediate/ (2 tunes), 03-original/ (1 tune), fingering-charts.svg, songsheet.pdf, README.md. Each tune folder has at least tune.abc, tune.mid, notes.md. The original tune has its melody filled in (not just a TODO scaffold), uses overtone-series-friendly intervals, and stays in the fujara's range (C3-C6). The build repo's main README has a 'Learn to play' section linking to the new folder.

assertions.name: every tune has tune.abc, tune.mid, notes.md
original tune has actual melody (not TODO)
all pitches in range (C3-C6) per registry
build repo README has 'Learn to play' section

evals.name: compose-original-naf-from-mood
evals.prompt: Compose an original Heifer Zephyr tune for an A-tuned 6-hole Native American flute. The mood I want: contemplative, slow, single-octave pentatonic, evocative of a pre-dawn river valley. AABA form. File it under sheet-music/catalog/original/ and run the full render pipeline so I get notation, MIDI, fingering chart, and a one-page songsheet.
evals.expected_output: A catalog/original/<slug>/ folder with tune.abc (containing actual notes, not the TODO scaffold), tune.mid, tune.svg or tune.ly, tune-fingering.svg, tune.pdf, notes.md. The melody fits A-minor pentatonic, stays within A4-D6 (the registry's range_low/high for naf-6hole), follows AABA structure (4 distinct sections, A repeated three times), uses sparse ornamentation appropriate for NAF, and the title is a simple two-or-three-word evocative name (not a clever pun).

assertions.name: tune.abc has actual notes (no TODO marker)
all pitches in NAF range A4-D6
AABA form is identifiable in the ABC

evals.name: transcribe-user-melody-for-floor-harp
evals.prompt: I'm humming a tune that goes 'do mi sol mi do, re fa la fa re, do' over four bars in 3/4 time, slow waltz feel. Arrange it for my floor harp (the one I built from the floor-harp repo) and add it to my learn-to-play/ folder there. Floor harp is in C major, no lever changes preferred for beginners.
evals.expected_output: A new tune folder inside floor-harp/learn-to-play/01-easy/ (or wherever the user's intent fits). tune.abc contains a transcription of the solfege C E G E C, D F A F D, C in 3/4 with a slow tempo. tune.mid plays back. Fingering chart is generated. notes.md explains the transcription, the key choice, and a practice tip. No lever changes added. The build repo's README is updated.

assertions.name: tune is in C major (no lever changes)
first phrase pitches match C E G E C
all pitches in floor harp range

notes: Three realistic test prompts covering the three core skill operations: (1) full Mode-A deposit, (2) original composition, (3) user-supplied melody transcription. Run with: python <skill-creator>/scripts/run_eval.py --eval-set evals/evals.json --skill-path . . The custom assertion kinds (tune-shape-valid, validate-arrangement, etc.) need a small grader script in scripts/grade_evals.py to check; for v0.1 the human-review pass through eval-viewer/generate_review.py is the primary signal.
